rationale: The ensures is `H ==> det_set_io_mapping_equal`, so H's conjuncts are NOT body-level hypotheses — the prior unconditional `assert(forall ...)` of an H-conjunct therefore failed. Wrapping the proof in `if H_relevant { ... }` records each inner assertion as `H_relevant ==> ...`, exactly the shape Verus needs when discharging the implication at exit. Inside the branch we prove `post1_self_.page_array@ == post2_self_.page_array@` pointwise (case-split on i == index: at i == index every Page field agrees via H's per-field ensures; otherwise mention both `post*_self_.page_array@[i]` to fire both foralls at the same i, yielding equality with `pre_self_.page_array@[i]`), then close with `=~=` and `wf()`-derived length. The remaining scalar/set/map fields of `det_set_io_mapping_equal` follow from the `post*.X == pre.X` conjuncts of H by transitivity at the ensures check.
